It is a genus within this family and is native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is also known as 'Water-ditch-grass' and is often found in wetlands and marshy areas. This plant is endemic to the Western Ghats in India. It is a relatively rare species and is not widely studied.

The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which help in treating ailments such as fever, respiratory issues, and skin infections.
